title: CIL Civil — Advanced Mock 12 (Conceptual)
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: As per Income Tax Department data (June 2026), India's net direct tax collections in early FY27 rose by about what percentage year-on-year?
questions.solution: Net direct tax collections rose ~14.6% YoY to about Rs 5.21 trillion.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, which country became the first Central Asian member of the New Development Bank (NDB)?
questions.solution: Uzbekistan became the first Central Asian nation to join the BRICS-backed NDB.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which India-born theoretical physicist won the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics?
questions.solution: Rajasthan-born physicist Jainendra K. Jain received the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution ranked first in the QS World University Rankings 2027 (released June 2026)?
questions.solution: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) ranked first in QS World University Rankings 2027.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In June 2026, which Indian jurist was elected a Judge of the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS)?
questions.solution: Indian jurist Bimal N. Patel was elected to ITLOS in June 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, PM Narendra Modi received the highest national award of which country?
questions.solution: PM Modi was conferred Slovakia's highest national award in 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026, in which an Indian Army contingent participated, was held in which country?
questions.solution: The multinational Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026 was held in Mongolia.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who was named the new Chairman of GIC Re in June 2026?
questions.solution: The government named Hitesh Joshi as the new Chairman of GIC Re.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, ONGC was assigned to develop a new strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) facility at which location?
questions.solution: ONGC was tasked with building a new underground crude oil SPR cavern at Mangaluru, Karnataka.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The India–UK CETA and Social Security Agreement were reported to take effect from which date?
questions.solution: Reports indicated the India–UK CETA would take effect from July 15, 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the currency of the United Kingdom?
questions.solution: The UK's currency is the Pound Sterling (£).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Where are the headquarters of the United Nations located?
questions.solution: The UN headquarters is located in New York City, USA.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Where is the headquarters of the World Health Organization (WHO)?
questions.solution: The WHO is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is India's national space agency?
questions.solution: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is India's space agency.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Taj Mahal is located in which city?
questions.solution: The Taj Mahal is in Agra, Uttar Pradesh, on the bank of the Yamuna.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Sun Temple at Konark is located in which state?
questions.solution: The Konark Sun Temple is in Odisha, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In which year did the Dandi (Salt) March take place?
questions.solution: Gandhi led the Dandi Salt March in 1930 during the Civil Disobedience Movement.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Quit India Movement was launched in which year?
questions.solution: The Quit India Movement began in August 1942.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who founded the Maurya Empire?
questions.solution: Chandragupta Maurya founded the Maurya Empire around 321 BCE.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: How many players are there in a cricket team on the field?
questions.solution: A cricket team fields eleven players.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Thomas Cup is associated with which sport?
questions.solution: The Thomas Cup is the premier men's international team badminton event.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Bharat Ratna is India's highest:
questions.solution: The Bharat Ratna is the highest civilian award of India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution regulates monetary policy in India?
questions.solution: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is the country's central bank and monetary authority.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: GST in India was implemented from which date?
questions.solution: The Goods and Services Tax came into force on 1 July 2017.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The speed of light in vacuum is approximately:
questions.solution: Light travels at about 3 × 10^8 metres per second in vacuum.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A invests Rs 5000 for 12 months and B invests Rs 6000 for 4 months. Out of a total profit of Rs 3000, A's share is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit is split in the ratio of capital × time invested by each partner.
**Formula.** $\text{share}_A=P\cdot\tfrac{x\,m_A}{x\,m_A+y\,m_B}$
**Given.** A: Rs 5000×12 mo; B: Rs 6000×4 mo; profit Rs 3000.
**Working.** Ratio $=60000:24000$; share$_A=3000\times\tfrac{60000}{84000}$.
**Answer.** A's share $=$ Rs 2143.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at 7:24 is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The hour hand sweeps 30° per hour and 0.5° per minute, the minute hand 6° per minute; take the smaller of the two angles.
**Formula.** $\theta=\left|30H-5.5M\right|$
**Given.** H = 7, M = 24.
**Working.** $=\left|30\times7-5.5\times24\right|=78^\circ$; smaller $=\min(78,360-78)$.
**Answer.** $\theta=78^\circ$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A pipe fills a tank in 4 h, but with a leak it takes 6 h. The leak alone can empty the full tank in:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The leak's rate is the fill-rate without the leak minus the slower fill-rate with the leak.
**Formula.** $T=\tfrac{ab}{b-a}$
**Given.** Fill without leak a = 4 h; with leak b = 6 h.
**Working.** Leak rate $=\tfrac1{4}-\tfrac1{6}$; emptying time $=\tfrac{4\times6}{6-4}$.
**Answer.** $T=12$ h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A vessel holds 80 L of milk. 8 L is drawn out and replaced with water; this is repeated 3 times. The milk remaining is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each draw-and-replace keeps the fraction $(1-x/V)$ of milk; after n repeats this fraction is raised to the n-th power.
**Formula.** $M=V\left(1-\tfrac{x}{V}\right)^{n}$
**Given.** V = 80 L, x = 8 L, n = 3.
**Working.** $=80\left(1-\tfrac{8}{80}\right)^{3}$.
**Answer.** $M\approx 58.32$ L.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The difference between compound and simple interest on Rs 20000 at 12% per annum for 2 years is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Over 2 years the gap between compound and simple interest is the first year's interest-on-interest.
**Formula.** $CI-SI=P\left(\tfrac{r}{100}\right)^2$
**Given.** P = Rs 20000, r = 12% p.a., t = 2 yr.
**Working.** $=20000\left(\tfrac{12}{100}\right)^2$.
**Answer.** $CI-SI=$ Rs 288.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A trader marks goods 50% above cost, then gives a 20% discount. His net profit percentage is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Net selling factor is the markup factor times the discount factor; profit% is that factor reduced by 1.
**Formula.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{m}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{d}{100}\right)$
**Given.** Markup m = 50%, discount d = 20%.
**Working.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{50}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{20}{100}\right)=1.2$.
**Answer.** Profit $=(f-1)\times100=20\%$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two trains 180 m and 200 m long move in opposite directions at 60 km/h and 48 km/h. The time to cross each other is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Opposite directions add speeds; convert km/h to m/s with the factor 5/18, then divide the combined length by the relative speed.
**Formula.** $t=\tfrac{L_1+L_2}{(s_1+s_2)\times\frac{5}{18}}$
**Given.** L₁ = 180 m, L₂ = 200 m, s₁ = 60 km/h, s₂ = 48 km/h.
**Working.** Relative $=(60+48)\times\tfrac{5}{18}=30$ m/s; $t=\tfrac{180+200}{30}$.
**Answer.** $t=12.67$ s.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A boat covers 48 km downstream in 4 h and the same 48 km upstream in 12 h. Its speed in still water is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Downstream speed adds the stream; upstream subtracts it. Still-water speed is the average of the two.
**Formula.** $u=\tfrac12\left[(b+s)+(b-s)\right]$
**Given.** Downstream 48 km in 4 h; upstream 48 km in 12 h.
**Working.** Downstream $=\tfrac{48}{4}=12$ km/h, upstream $=\tfrac{48}{12}=4$ km/h.
**Answer.** Still-water speed $u=\tfrac{12+4}{2}=8$ km/h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A invests Rs 5000 for 12 months and B invests Rs 8000 for 6 months. Out of a total profit of Rs 3000, A's share is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit is split in the ratio of capital × time invested by each partner.
**Formula.** $\text{share}_A=P\cdot\tfrac{x\,m_A}{x\,m_A+y\,m_B}$
**Given.** A: Rs 5000×12 mo; B: Rs 8000×6 mo; profit Rs 3000.
**Working.** Ratio $=60000:48000$; share$_A=3000\times\tfrac{60000}{108000}$.
**Answer.** A's share $=$ Rs 1667.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The probability of getting an even number on a single throw of a fair die is:
questions.solution: Count the favourable outcomes out of 6 equally likely faces to get 1/2.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The LCM of 9 and 12 is:
questions.solution: The lowest common multiple of 9 and 12 is 36.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A sum doubles itself in 12.5 years under simple interest. The annual rate is:
questions.solution: To double, SI = P, so rate = 100/T = 100/12.5 = 8%.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Successive discounts of 20% and 20% on a marked price of Rs 1500 give a final price of:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A and B invest in the ratio 5 : 6. Out of a total profit of Rs 11000, A's share is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 99 is divided in the ratio 4 : 5, the larger part is:
questions.solution: Larger part = 99 × 5/9 = 55.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Simple interest on Rs 4000 at 6% per annum for 2 years is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: What is the average of the first 16 natural numbers?
questions.solution: Average of first n naturals = (n+1)/2 = (16+1)/2 = 8.5.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The percentage increase when a value rises from 60 to 70 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: 3 kg of rice at Rs 20/kg is mixed with 3 kg at Rs 50/kg. The average price per kg is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: What is 12% of 480?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A man goes at 40 km/h and returns over the same route at 90 km/h. His average speed is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A 200 m long train crosses a pole in 20 seconds. Its speed is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 40% of a number is 90, the number is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The square root of 2025 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A number divided by 7 gives quotient 13 and remainder 4. The number is:
questions.solution: Number = divisor × quotient + remainder = 7×13+4 = 95.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A is the brother of B. B is the brother of C. C is the father of D. How is A related to D?
questions.solution: Tracing each relationship step by step gives: Uncle.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A = 1, B = 2, … , Z = 26, what is the sum of the letter values of 'FAD'?
questions.solution: Add the positional values of F, A, D to get 11.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 6 km towards north, then turns east and walks 8 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.solution: Straight-line distance = √(6² + 8²) = √100 = 10 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: No A is B. All B are C. Which conclusion necessarily follows?
questions.solution: Applying the rules of categorical syllogism, the valid conclusion is: Some C are not A.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a certain code, each letter of a word is moved two steps forward in the English alphabet. The code for 'DOG' is:
questions.solution: Replace every letter by the one two places ahead: DOG → FQI.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term in the series: 4, 9, 19, 39, 79, ?
questions.solution: Each term = previous × 2 + 1, so the next term = 79 × 2 + 1 = 159.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A = 1, B = 2, … , Z = 26, what is the sum of the letter values of 'ACE'?
questions.solution: Add the positional values of A, C, E to get 9.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 9 km towards north, then turns east and walks 12 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.solution: Straight-line distance = √(9² + 12²) = √225 = 15 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: Rose, Lily, Lotus, Mango?
questions.solution: Mango belongs to a different category from the rest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.solution: The only daughter of her mother is the woman herself, so she is the man's mother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 3, 7, 19, 25?
questions.solution: 3, 7, 19 are prime; 25 is composite.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 9 km north, then 12 km east. His distance from the start is:
questions.solution: Resultant = √(9² + 12²) = 15 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A=1, B=2, ... then the sum of the letters of CAB is:
questions.solution: Add the alphabet positions of C, A, B to get 6.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 4, 9, 16, 25, ?
questions.solution: These are perfect squares; next = 6² = 36.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.solution: Each number is halved, so 20 → 10.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: GH, JK, MN, ?
questions.solution: Each pair skips one letter; after MN comes PQ.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If each letter is replaced by the next letter, GOLD is coded as:
questions.solution: Shift each letter forward by one to get HPME.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 27, 64, 100, 125?
questions.solution: The others are perfect cubes; 100 is not.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row of 20 people, X is 7th from the left. X's position from the right is:
questions.solution: Position from right = 20 − 7 + 1 = 14.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: All cats are animals. All animals breathe. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution: Both premises are universal affirmatives, so the chained conclusion 'All cats breathe' follows.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A person facing west turns 90° to the left. Now they face:
questions.solution: Turning left from west leads to South.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: The angle between the hands of a clock at 3:00 is:
questions.solution: Each hour = 30°; at 3:00 the hands are 3 × 30 = 90° apart.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Bird : Nest :: Bee : ?
questions.solution: Just as Nest relates to Bird, Hive relates to Bee.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If 2 January is a Monday, what day is 9 January?
questions.solution: 9 − 2 = 7 days, exactly one week, so it is again Monday.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correct meaning of the word 'Frugal'.
questions.solution: 'Frugal' means careful with money, i.e. economical.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Abundant'.
questions.solution: 'Abundant' means existing in large quantity, i.e. plentiful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Brief'.
questions.solution: 'Brief' means short or concise.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Happy'.
questions.solution: 'Happy' is synonymous with 'joyful'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Enormous'.
questions.solution: 'Enormous' means very large, i.e. huge.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Diligent'.
questions.solution: 'Diligent' describes someone hard-working and careful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Candid'.
questions.solution: 'Candid' means open and frank in speech.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Vivid'.
questions.solution: 'Vivid' means bright, clear and intense.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Ancient'.
questions.solution: 'Ancient' (very old) is opposite to 'modern'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Victory'.
questions.solution: 'Victory' is opposite to 'defeat'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Expand'.
questions.solution: 'Expand' is opposite to 'contract'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Generous'.
questions.solution: 'Generous' is opposite to 'stingy' (mean with money).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Transparent'.
questions.solution: 'Transparent' (see-through) is opposite to 'opaque'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Optimist'.
questions.solution: An optimist's opposite is a 'pessimist'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correctly spelt word.
questions.solution: The correct spelling is 'Occasion' (one c, two s).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'She is good ___ mathematics.'
questions.solution: The idiom is 'good at' a subject or skill.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He has been living here ___ 2010.'
questions.solution: 'Since' is used with a point of time like a year.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Divide this ___ two parts.'
questions.solution: 'Divide into' is the correct collocation for separation.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He is married ___ a doctor.'
questions.solution: The correct collocation is 'married to' someone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The book is ___ the table.'
questions.solution: 'On' indicates resting on a surface.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a person who cannot read or write':
questions.solution: Such a person is 'illiterate'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The study of living organisms is called:
questions.solution: 'Biology' is the study of all living organisms.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One who studies stars and celestial bodies is an:
questions.solution: An 'astronomer' scientifically studies celestial bodies.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a speech made without preparation':
questions.solution: An unprepared speech is delivered 'extempore'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: A place where birds are kept is called:
questions.solution: An 'aviary' houses birds; an apiary keeps bees.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flow in a circular pipe is laminar when the Reynolds number is:
questions.solution: Pipe flow is laminar for Re below roughly 2000.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As the depth of flow in a prismatic open channel approaches the critical depth at a fixed discharge, the specific energy:
questions.solution: For a given discharge the specific-energy curve has a minimum exactly at critical depth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The superelevation for a design speed of 80 km/h and radius 200 m (e = V²/127R) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A total station essentially combines a theodolite with:
questions.solution: A total station integrates an electronic theodolite with an EDM and data recorder.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a singly reinforced section (Fe415, M25 concrete, width 300 mm, effective depth 500 mm), the limiting moment of resistance (Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d²) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a balanced (limiting) Fe415 section the moment of resistance is fixed by the coefficient 0.138 in $f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$.
**Formula.** $M_{u,lim}=0.138\,f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$
**Given.** f_ck = 25 MPa, b = 300 mm, d = 500 mm.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.138\times25\times300\times500^2}{10^6}$ kNm.
**Answer.** $M_{u,lim}\approx 258.8$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The actual size of a standard modular brick (without mortar) is:
questions.solution: The actual modular brick size is 190 × 90 × 90 mm; nominal size with mortar is 200 × 100 × 100 mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The centre of pressure on a vertical immersed plane surface lies:
questions.solution: Due to increasing pressure with depth, the centre of pressure is below the centroid.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3 m clay layer (Cc = 0.4, e₀ = 1) under an initial effective stress of 120 kPa receives a 80 kPa increment. The primary consolidation settlement is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Primary consolidation of a normally consolidated clay follows the logarithmic compression law.
**Formula.** $S_c=\tfrac{C_c H}{1+e_0}\log_{10}\tfrac{\sigma_0+\Delta\sigma}{\sigma_0}$
**Given.** C_c = 0.4, H = 3 m, e₀ = 1, σ₀ = 120 kPa, Δσ = 80 kPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.4\times3000}{1+1}\log_{10}\tfrac{120+80}{120}$ mm.
**Answer.** $S_c\approx 133.1$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about a hydraulic jump in an open channel is correct?
options: It converts subcritical flow to supercritical.
It can occur only in closed pipes.
It dissipates energy while changing supercritical flow to subcritical.
questions.solution: A hydraulic jump takes fast, shallow supercritical flow to slow, deep subcritical flow with a significant loss of energy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If E = 200 GPa and Poisson's ratio = 0.3, the modulus of rigidity G is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The process of determining the relative elevations of points is called:
questions.solution: Levelling determines the relative heights (elevations) of points.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The broad gauge of Indian Railways measures:
questions.solution: Indian Broad Gauge is 1676 mm; 1435 mm is standard gauge.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The thermal stress developed in a fully restrained bar for a temperature rise ΔT is:
questions.solution: Restrained thermal stress = αEΔT, independent of length and area.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The number of links in a 30 m metric chain is:
questions.solution: A 30 m metric chain has 150 links of 0.2 m each.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point the major and minor principal stresses are 100 MPa and 40 MPa. From Mohr's circle, the maximum in-plane shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum in-plane shear stress equals the radius of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\tau_{max}=\tfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 100 MPa, σ₃ = 40 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{100-40}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\tau_{max}=30$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tacheometry is mainly used to determine:
options: Horizontal distances and elevations without chaining
questions.solution: Tacheometry measures distances and elevations rapidly using stadia readings.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The final setting time of ordinary Portland cement should not exceed:
questions.solution: IS limits the final setting time of OPC to 600 minutes (10 hours).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The detention time of a tank of volume 100 m³ treating a flow of 25 m³/h is:
questions.solution: Detention time = Volume / Discharge = 100 / 25 = 4 hours.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The strain energy stored per unit volume under axial stress σ is:
questions.solution: Resilience (strain energy density) = σ²/2E.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An axial load of 100 kN acts on a bar of cross-sectional area 800 mm². The direct stress is:
questions.solution: Stress = P/A = 100000 N / 800 mm² = 125 MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The initial setting time of ordinary Portland cement should not be less than:
questions.solution: IS specifies a minimum initial setting time of 30 minutes for OPC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Superelevation is provided on:
questions.solution: Superelevation counters centrifugal force on horizontal curves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using Manning's formula with n = 0.015, hydraulic radius R = 0.8 m and slope S = 0.001, the mean velocity is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Manning's equation gives mean velocity from roughness, hydraulic radius and bed slope.
**Formula.** $V=\tfrac1n R^{2/3} S^{1/2}$
**Given.** n = 0.015, R = 0.8 m, S = 0.001.
**Working.** $=\tfrac1{0.015}\times0.8^{2/3}\times\sqrt{0.001}$.
**Answer.** $V\approx 1.82$ m/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stopping sight distance depends on the driver's reaction time and the:
questions.solution: SSD = lag (reaction) distance + braking distance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a singly reinforced section (Fe415, M20 concrete, width 300 mm, effective depth 550 mm), the limiting moment of resistance (Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d²) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a balanced (limiting) Fe415 section the moment of resistance is fixed by the coefficient 0.138 in $f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$.
**Formula.** $M_{u,lim}=0.138\,f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$
**Given.** f_ck = 20 MPa, b = 300 mm, d = 550 mm.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.138\times20\times300\times550^2}{10^6}$ kNm.
**Answer.** $M_{u,lim}\approx 250.5$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slump test on fresh concrete measures its:
questions.solution: The slump test is a measure of the workability (consistency) of fresh concrete.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The combined correction for curvature and refraction in levelling (d in km) is about:
questions.solution: Curvature (−0.0785 d²) and refraction (+0.0112 d²) combine to about −0.0673 d² m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever 4 m long carries a 8 kN load at its free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM at fixed end = W × L = 8 × 4 = 32 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rigid pavements are generally made of:
questions.solution: Rigid pavements use a cement concrete slab that resists loads in flexure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point the major and minor principal stresses are 140 MPa and 40 MPa. From Mohr's circle, the maximum in-plane shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum in-plane shear stress equals the radius of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\tau_{max}=\tfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 140 MPa, σ₃ = 40 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{140-40}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\tau_{max}=50$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The length of a transition curve based on the allowable rate of change of centrifugal acceleration varies with the design speed v as:
questions.solution: Ls = v³/(C·R), so the comfort-based transition length grows with the cube of speed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a beam section subjected to bending, the bending stress is zero at the:
questions.solution: Bending stress varies linearly and is zero at the neutral axis (centroidal axis).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If the fore bearing of a line is 75°, its back bearing is:
questions.solution: Back bearing = fore bearing ± 180° = 255°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The average domestic per-capita water demand in Indian cities is taken as about:
questions.solution: A domestic demand of about 135 lpcd is commonly assumed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section modulus of a rectangular section 100 mm wide and 450 mm deep is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The coagulant most commonly used in water treatment is:
questions.solution: Alum (aluminium sulphate) is the usual coagulant for removing fine suspended matter.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The fundamental principle of surveying is to work:
options: From the part to the whole
From the whole to the part
questions.solution: Surveying proceeds from whole to part to control accumulation of errors.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a simply supported beam with a central point load of 12 kN, the maximum shear force is:
questions.solution: Each reaction = W/2 = 6 kN, which is the maximum shear force.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a floating body, stable equilibrium requires the metacentric height to be:
questions.solution: A positive metacentric height (M above G) ensures stable equilibrium.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge through a section of area 2.5 m² with velocity 4 m/s is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The permissible pH range of drinking water as per IS 10500 is:
questions.solution: IS 10500 specifies a desirable pH of 6.5 to 8.5 for drinking water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: First-order BOD kinetics means the rate of oxidation is proportional to the:
questions.solution: BOD follows first-order kinetics, so the rate is proportional to the organic matter still remaining.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If LL = 55% and PL = 30%, the plasticity index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Manning's formula is used for:
questions.solution: Manning's equation estimates uniform flow velocity in open channels.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 8 m carries a central point load of 30 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 30 kN, L = 8 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{30\times8}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=60$ kNm at mid-span.

figure.caption: Simply supported beam — central point load

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Block shear failure of a bolted tension member involves:
options: Tension rupture on one plane and shear rupture on another
questions.solution: Block shear tears out a block by tension on one plane and shear on the perpendicular plane.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Terzaghi's bearing capacity factor Nc for φ = 0 (strip footing) is:
questions.solution: For φ = 0, Terzaghi's Nc = 5.7.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 15 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 15 kN/m, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{15\times4^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=30$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On a two-lane highway, the stopping sight distance compared with the overtaking (passing) sight distance is:
questions.solution: Overtaking sight distance must cover the whole passing manoeuvre against opposing traffic, so it is several times the stopping sight distance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A beam has overall depth 400 mm, clear cover 20 mm and 16 mm main bars. The effective depth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mechanical extra widening on a horizontal curve is provided predominantly on the:
questions.solution: Off-tracking sweeps the rear wheels toward the inside of the curve, so the mechanical component of extra widening is given on the inner side.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sedimentation tanks in water treatment primarily remove:
questions.solution: Sedimentation removes settleable suspended solids by gravity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 15 kPa and friction angle φ = 35°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 120 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 15 kPa, φ = 35°, σ = 120 kPa.
**Working.** $=15+120\times\tan 35^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=99.02$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an open channel, the specific energy is minimum at the:
questions.solution: The specific energy curve attains its minimum value at the critical depth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectangular channel 4 m wide carries 12 m³/s. The critical depth (yc = (q²/g)^{1/3}) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Critical depth in a rectangular channel depends only on the discharge per unit width q.
**Formula.** $y_c=\left(\tfrac{q^2}{g}\right)^{1/3}$
**Given.** Q = 12 m³/s, B = 4 m, g = 9.81 m/s².
**Working.** $q=\tfrac{12}{4}=3$ m²/s; $y_c=\left(\tfrac{3^2}{9.81}\right)^{1/3}$.
**Answer.** $y_c\approx 0.97$ m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Traffic volume is expressed as:
options: Number of vehicles per unit time
questions.solution: Traffic volume is the number of vehicles crossing a section per unit time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The activated sludge process is used for the treatment of:
questions.solution: The activated sludge process is an aerobic biological treatment for sewage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pin-ended column (E = 200 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴, length 4 m) has an Euler buckling load of about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A pin-ended column buckles at the Euler critical load with effective length equal to the actual length.
**Formula.** $P_{cr}=\tfrac{\pi^2 E I}{L^2}$
**Given.** E = 200 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{\pi^2\times200\times10^9\times16\times10^{-6}}{4^2}$ N.
**Answer.** $P_{cr}\approx 1973.9$ kN.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The water-cement ratio law was proposed by:
questions.solution: Abrams' law relates concrete strength inversely to the water-cement ratio.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries two equal 15 kN point loads placed 1 m from each support. The maximum bending moment (constant between the loads) is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two equal symmetric loads give equal reactions; between the loads the bending moment is constant at $R\cdot a$.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=P\,a$
**Given.** P = 15 kN, a = 1 m, span = 6 m.
**Working.** Each reaction $=15$ kN; $M_{max}=15\times1$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=15$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Contour lines on a map join points of:
questions.solution: A contour line connects points of equal elevation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The cement compound that liberates the greatest heat of hydration is:
questions.solution: C3A hydrates fastest and releases the most heat of hydration.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries a central point load of 40 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 40 kN, L = 6 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{40\times6}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=60$ kNm at mid-span.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 165" width="320" height="165" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="40" y1="55" x2="280" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="3"/><polygon points="40,55 31,72 49,72" fill="#64748b"/><polygon points="280,55 271,72 289,72" fill="#64748b"/><line x1="160" y1="16" x2="160" y2="46" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><polygon points="160,53 154,42 166,42" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="160" y="12" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">W = 40 kN</text><text x="160" y="88" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">span L = 6 m</text><polygon points="40,112 160,144 280,112" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.12)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="160" y="159" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">BMD — Mmax = 60 kNm</text></svg>
figure.caption: Simply supported beam — central point load

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy grade line lies above the hydraulic grade line by an amount equal to the:
questions.solution: EGL = HGL + velocity head (V²/2g).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point σx = 80 MPa, σy = 60 MPa and τxy = 20 MPa. The major principal stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The major principal stress is the centre of Mohr's circle plus its radius.
**Formula.** $\sigma_1=\tfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}+\sqrt{\left(\tfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$
**Given.** σx = 80 MPa, σy = 60 MPa, τxy = 20 MPa.
**Working.** Centre $=70$, radius $=22.36$; $\sigma_1=70+22.36$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_1=92.36$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) indicates:
questions.solution: BOD measures the oxygen needed to biodegrade organic matter, indicating organic pollution.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The instrument used to measure horizontal and vertical angles is the:
questions.solution: A theodolite measures both horizontal and vertical angles.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Rankine active earth pressure coefficient Ka for a soil with φ = 36° is:
questions.solution: Ka = (1−sinφ)/(1+sinφ) = 0.26 for φ = 36°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The coefficient of discharge Cd of an orifice equals:
questions.solution: Cd is the product of the coefficient of contraction and coefficient of velocity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principal stresses at a point are 50 MPa and 10 MPa. The normal stress on the plane carrying the maximum shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On the plane of maximum shear the normal stress equals the centre of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\sigma_n=\tfrac{\sigma_1+\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 50 MPa, σ₃ = 10 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{50+10}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_n=30$ MPa.

figure.caption: Centre of Mohr's circle = mean normal stress

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A hydraulic jump in an open channel forms when the flow changes from:
questions.solution: A hydraulic jump dissipates energy as the flow shifts from supercritical to subcritical.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The method best suited for analysing trusses with a single redundant member is the:
questions.solution: Consistent deformation (unit load) handles internally redundant trusses efficiently.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of length 2 m carries a 5 kN load at its free end (E = 200 GPa, I = 8×10⁻⁶ m⁴). The tip deflection (WL³/3EI) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An end-loaded cantilever has tip deflection $W L^3/3EI$.
**Formula.** $\delta=\tfrac{W L^3}{3 E I}$
**Given.** W = 5 kN, L = 2 m, E = 200 GPa, I = 8×10⁻⁶ m⁴.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{5\times10^3\times2^3}{3\times200\times10^9\times8\times10^{-6}}$ m.
**Answer.** $\delta\approx 8.3$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Camber on a road surface is provided mainly for:
questions.solution: Camber lets rainwater drain off the carriageway to the sides.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a fixed beam carrying a UDL w over span L, the support (fixed-end) moment is:
questions.solution: Fixed-end moment for a UDL is wL²/12 at each support.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a simply supported beam under a central point load, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: The bending moment is maximum at midspan.
The deflection is maximum at midspan.
The maximum bending stress occurs at the neutral axis.
The shear force is zero at midspan.
questions.solution: Bending stress varies linearly with distance from the neutral axis — it is zero at the NA and maximum at the extreme fibre, so that statement is false.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Poisson's ratio for structural steel is approximately:
questions.solution: Steel has a Poisson's ratio of about 0.3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RL of a benchmark is 120 m. With back sight 1.5 m and fore sight 2.2 m, the RL of the next point is:
questions.solution: RL = 120 + 1.5 − 2.2 = 119.3 m (rise and fall).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Disinfection of municipal water supplies is most commonly achieved by:
questions.solution: Chlorination is the standard method for disinfecting drinking water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 0 kPa and friction angle φ = 30°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 80 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 0 kPa, φ = 30°, σ = 80 kPa.
**Working.** $=0+80\times\tan 30^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=46.19$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Regarding superelevation on a highway curve, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: It is achieved by raising the outer edge relative to the inner edge.
IRC limits it to about 7% in plain and rolling terrain.
It is provided primarily to drain rainwater off the carriageway.
It counteracts the centrifugal force on a vehicle.
questions.solution: Superelevation counters centrifugal force; surface drainage is the job of the (separate) camber, so the drainage claim is false.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Curing of concrete is done primarily to:
questions.solution: Curing maintains moisture so cement hydration continues and concrete gains strength.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 3 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 5 kN/m over its entire length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a cantilever produces its maximum moment at the fixed support.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{2}$
**Given.** w = 5 kN/m, L = 3 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{5\times3^2}{2}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=22.5$ kNm at the fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Closely spaced contour lines indicate:
questions.solution: The closer the contours, the steeper the ground slope.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a void ratio e = 0.75, the porosity n is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a well-graded soil, the coefficient of curvature Cc lies between:
questions.solution: Well-graded soils have a high uniformity coefficient and Cc between 1 and 3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hardness in water is caused mainly by salts of:
questions.solution: Dissolved calcium and magnesium salts are the main cause of hardness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The velocity head corresponding to a velocity of 5 m/s is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The CBR test is used in the design of:
questions.solution: California Bearing Ratio is used to design flexible pavement thickness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pin-ended column (E = 200 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴, length 5 m) has an Euler buckling load of about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A pin-ended column buckles at the Euler critical load with effective length equal to the actual length.
**Formula.** $P_{cr}=\tfrac{\pi^2 E I}{L^2}$
**Given.** E = 200 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴, L = 5 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{\pi^2\times200\times10^9\times16\times10^{-6}}{5^2}$ N.
**Answer.** $P_{cr}\approx 1263.3$ kN.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If the design speed is doubled, the braking-distance component of the stopping sight distance:
questions.solution: Braking distance = v²/(2gf) varies with the square of speed, so doubling speed quadruples it.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A venturimeter is used to measure:
questions.solution: A venturimeter measures discharge using the pressure difference between throat and inlet.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge over a rectangular weir (Cd = 0.62) of length 2 m under a head of 0.4 m is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Discharge over a sharp-crested rectangular weir varies with the 3/2 power of the head.
**Formula.** $Q=\tfrac23 C_d\sqrt{2g}\,L\,H^{3/2}$
**Given.** C_d = 0.62, L = 2 m, H = 0.4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac23\times0.62\times\sqrt{2\times9.81}\times2\times0.4^{1.5}$.
**Answer.** $Q\approx 0.93$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a balanced section with Fe415 steel, the limiting neutral axis depth factor xu,max/d is:
questions.solution: IS 456 specifies xu,max/d = 0.48 for Fe415 steel.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The cross-sectional area of one 16 mm diameter bar is approximately:
questions.solution: Area = π/4 × 16² = 201 mm².

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Extra widening of a carriageway on a horizontal curve accounts for:
options: Off-tracking of rear wheels plus a psychological allowance
questions.solution: Extra widening covers mechanical off-tracking and a psychological width allowance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Marshall stability test is used for:
questions.solution: The Marshall test determines optimum bitumen content in bituminous mixes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principal stresses at a point are 70 MPa and 30 MPa. The normal stress on the plane carrying the maximum shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On the plane of maximum shear the normal stress equals the centre of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\sigma_n=\tfrac{\sigma_1+\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 70 MPa, σ₃ = 30 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{70+30}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_n=50$ MPa.

figure.caption: Centre of Mohr's circle = mean normal stress

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A prismatic compass measures bearings in the:
questions.solution: A prismatic compass reads whole circle bearings from 0° to 360°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point σx = 120 MPa, σy = 20 MPa and τxy = 40 MPa. The major principal stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The major principal stress is the centre of Mohr's circle plus its radius.
**Formula.** $\sigma_1=\tfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}+\sqrt{\left(\tfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$
**Given.** σx = 120 MPa, σy = 20 MPa, τxy = 40 MPa.
**Working.** Centre $=70$, radius $=64.03$; $\sigma_1=70+64.03$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_1=134.03$ MPa.
